data.body:
COMPLETED. Capture helper across all three interfaces via new src/rebar/_commands/session_log.py:
- append(entry, *, summary, relates_to, discovered_from) creates a session_log on first use (titled by summary) and appends to the SAME log thereafter; start() rotates to a fresh log. 'Current' log tracked by a LOCAL git-ignored pointer <repo>/.rebar/current_session_log (added .rebar/ to .gitignore), so stateless CLI invocations in a checkout converge on one log. All writes via composer.create_core/_seam.append_event/composer.link_core (inherits add5 rules).
- Library: rebar.append_session_log + rebar.start_session_log.
- CLI: rebar session-log append "<entry>" / start (in _ARGV_REGISTRY + _WRITES_FULL) + help/overview/goldens.
- MCP: write-gated log_session(entry) (absent under REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1).

Tests (tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_session_log_capture.py, 10 cases): create-then-append, exactly-one-log-on-first-use, start-rotation, relates_to allowed + blocks refused, empty-entry refusal, library==CLI==MCP convergence on the current log, readonly gating.

Full offline tier: 2095 passed (the single error was a concurrent-commit HEAD-move false positive from a parallel agent landing 0b32/7d1d on main mid-run; passes in isolation). ruff clean. NOTE: rebased cleanly on top of concurrent commits 0b32 (canonical event-byte) + 7d1d (HLC).
